[Project README: /home/ishanp/Documents/GitHub/automaton/README.md]
# Automaton — AI-Native Rust Automation Substrate

**Automaton** is a CLI-based, graph-native automation framework for AI agents to create, compose, and execute modular Rust workflows. It exposes its entire substrate through an MCP server built on the official [rmcp](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/rust-sdk) crate.

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    AI Agent (LLM)                        │
│  Uses MCP tools to create modules → plan → execute       │
└──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                       │ MCP (stdio/SSE)
┌──────────────────────▼──────────────────────────────────┐
│                automaton-mcp (MCP Server)                │
│  module.* | workflow.* | graph.* | registry.* | run.*   │
└──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                       │
┌──────────────────────▼──────────────────────────────────┐
│                automaton-engine (Orchestrator)           │
│  Planner → Materializer → Executor                      │
│  Converts design graph → run DAG → execution            │
└──────┬──────────────────────────┬───────────────────────┘
       │                          │
┌──────▼──────────┐    ┌──────────▼───────────────────┐
│ automaton-graph │    │ automaton-runtime             │
│ Property graph   │    │ Process sandbox, retry, I/O │
│ (SQLite-backed)  │    │                              │
└─────────────────┘    └──────────────────────────────┘
       │                          │
       └──────────┬───────────────┘
                  │
┌─────────────────▼───────────────────────────────────┐
│                automaton-registry                    │
│  SQLite-backed module catalog + content-addressed    │
│  build cache + run history                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Quick Start

```bash
# Initialize workspace
automaton init

# Scaffold a module
automaton new github.issue_triage

# Build it
automaton build github.issue_triage

# Run it
automaton run github.issue_triage --input '{"repo": "user/repo"}'

# Plan a workflow from a module
automaton plan github.issue_triage --max-depth 5

# List modules
automaton list

# Show module details
automaton show github.issue_triage

# Inspect the graph
automaton graph nodes
automaton graph path module_a module_b

# Start MCP server (for AI agents)
automaton mcp

# Diagnostics
automaton doctor
```

## Workspace Layout

```
~/.local/share/automaton/     # Data directory
├── registry.db               # Module catalog (SQLite)
├── graph.db                  # Property graph store (SQLite)
├── builds/                   # Compiled binary cache
├── modules/                  # Uncompiled module sources
├── work/                     # Runtime working directory
└── tmp/                      # Temp execution artifacts
```

## Project Structure

```
crates/
├── automaton-core/           # Shared types: manifests, graph nodes, errors
├── automaton-sdk/            # #[automaton] proc macro + prelude
├── automaton-sdk-derive/     # Proc macro implementation
├── automaton-cli/            # CLI binary
├── automaton-engine/         # Planner, DAG materializer, executor
├── automaton-registry/       # SQLite-backed module + build + run DB
├── automaton-graph/          # SQLite-backed property graph store
├── automaton-mcp/            # MCP server (rmcp)
└── automaton-runtime/        # Child process runner, retry, timeout
```

## Graph Model

Two-layer architecture:

1. **Design Graph** (persistent property graph): Modules, Workflows, Triggers, Resources, Secrets, Capabilities — interconnected via labeled edges (`DEPENDS_ON`, `CALLS`, `TRIGGERS`, `USES_RESOURCE`, etc.)

2. **Run Graph** (materialized DAG for one execution): Compiled from design graph + context, verified acyclic via `petgraph::toposort`.

## MCP Surface (for AI agents)

| Tool                   | Description                            |
| ---------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| `module.create`        | Register a new automation module       |
| `module.build`         | Compile module to binary               |
| `module.validate`      | Validate manifest + source             |
| `module.run`           | Execute a module                       |
| `module.deprecate`     | Remove module                          |
| `workflow.plan`        | Discover dependencies, build run graph |
| `workflow.materialize` | Verify DAG validity (cycle check)      |
| `graph.query`          | Query nodes by kind                    |
| `graph.pathfind`       | Find paths between nodes               |
| `graph.add_edge`       | Wire module dependencies               |
| `registry.search`      | Search registered modules              |
| `resource.bind`        | Bind typed resources                   |
| `run.logs`             | Inspect run history                    |
| `run.retry`            | Retry failed execution                 |
| `system.health`        | Component health check                 |

## Module Authoring

```rust
use automaton_sdk::prelude::*;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Deserialize, JsonSchema)]
struct Input {
    repo: String,
    issue_number: u32,
}

#[derive(Serialize, JsonSchema)]
struct Output {
    summary: String,
    priority: String,
}

#[automaton]
async fn main(ctx: Context, input: Input) -> anyhow::Result<Output> {
    // Your automation logic
    Ok(Output {
        summary: format!("Triaged issue #{} from {}", input.issue_number, input.repo),
        priority: "medium".to_string(),
    })
}
```

Manifest: `automation.yaml`

```yaml
name: github.issue_triage
version: 0.1.0
entry: main
summary: "Triage GitHub issues by priority"
timeout_ms: 30000
retry:
  max_attempts: 3
  delay_ms: 1000
  backoff: exponential
permissions:
  - github.read
resources:
  - github.api
depends_on:
  - llm.summarize
tags:
  - github
  - issue
  - triage
```

## Design Decisions

- **Rust-first**: Smallest binary size (~2.7 MB compiled) and runtime memory (~4 MB RSS)
- **SQLite-backed**: No Postgres dependency for local-first use
- **petgraph DAG**: Topological scheduling with cycle detection
- **Windmill-inspired packaging**: Code + YAML manifest, content-addressed builds
- **MCP-native control**: AI agents interact through the MCP protocol, not raw shell
- **Property graph**: Two-layer (design + run) enables recursive module composition
- **Incremental compilation**: Shared build cache, debug/release mode split

## License

MIT

1: use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};
2: use std::path::Path;
3: use std::sync::Mutex;
4: 
5: use automaton_core::*;
6: 
7: use rusqlite::Connection;
8: use serde::Serialize;
9: 
10: /// Persistent property graph store backed by SQLite.
11: pub struct GraphStore {
12:     db: Mutex<Connection>,
13: }
14: 
15: fn with_db<T>(db: &Mutex<Connection>, f: impl FnOnce(&Connection) -> Result<T>) -> Result<T> {
16:     let conn = db
17:         .lock()
18:         .map_err(|e| AutomatonError::Database(e.to_string()))?;
19:     f(&conn)
20: }
21: 
22: impl GraphStore {
23:     /// Open the graph store from the dedicated graph.db file.
24:     /// Kept for backward compatibility. Prefer `open_merged` for new installations.
25:     pub fn open(data_dir: &Path) -> Result<Self> {
26:         let db_path = data_dir.join("graph.db");
27:         let db = Connection::open(&db_path)?;
28:         let store = Self { db: Mutex::new(db) };
29:         store.init_tables()?;
30:         Ok(store)
31:     }
32: 
33:     /// Open the graph store using registry.db, which now contains merged
34:     /// graph_nodes and graph_edges tables alongside all registry tables.
35:     /// This eliminates the separate graph.db file.
36:     pub fn open_merged(data_dir: &Path) -> Result<Self> {
37:         let db_path = data_dir.join("registry.db");
38:         let db = Connection::open(&db_path)?;
39:         Ok(Self { db: Mutex::new(db) })
40:     }
41: 
42:     fn init_tables(&self) -> Result<()> {
43:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
44:             db.execute_batch(
45:                 "
46:                 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS nodes (
47:                     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
48:                     kind TEXT NOT NULL,
49:                     name TEXT NOT NULL,
50:                     properties TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
51:                     created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
52:                 );
53:                 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS edges (
54:                     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
55:                     source TEXT NOT NULL,
56:                     target TEXT NOT NULL,
57:                     kind TEXT NOT NULL,
58:                     properties TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
59:                     created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
60:                     FOREIGN KEY (source) REFERENCES nodes(id),
61:                     FOREIGN KEY (target) REFERENCES nodes(id)
62:                 );
63:                 CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_edges_source ON edges(source);
64:                 CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_edges_target ON edges(target);
65:                 CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_edges_kind ON edges(kind);
66:                 CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_nodes_kind ON nodes(kind);
67:                 ",
68:             )?;
69:             Ok(())
70:         })
71:     }
72: 
73:     pub fn add_node(
74:         &self,
75:         kind: NodeKind,
76:         name: &str,
77:         properties: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,
78:     ) -> Result<String> {
79:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
80:             let id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
81:             let kind_str = serde_json::to_string(&kind)?;
82:             let props_str = serde_json::to_string(&properties)?;
83:             db.execute(
84:                 "INSERT INTO nodes (id, kind, name, properties) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4)",
85:                 rusqlite::params![id, kind_str, name, props_str],
86:             )?;
87:             Ok(id)
88:         })
89:     }
90: 
91:     pub fn add_edge(
92:         &self,
93:         source: &str,
94:         target: &str,
95:         kind: EdgeKind,
96:         properties: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,
97:     ) -> Result<String> {
98:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
99:             let id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
100:             let kind_str = serde_json::to_string(&kind)?;
101:             let props_str = serde_json::to_string(&properties)?;
102:             db.execute(
103:                 "INSERT INTO edges (id, source, target, kind, properties) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5)",
104:                 rusqlite::params![id, source, target, kind_str, props_str],
105:             )?;
106:             Ok(id)
107:         })
108:     }
109: 
110:     pub fn get_node(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Option<Node>> {
111:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
112:             let mut stmt = db.prepare(
113:                 "SELECT id, kind, name, properties, created_at FROM nodes WHERE id = ?1",
114:             )?;
115:             let rows = stmt.query_map(rusqlite::params![id], row_to_node)?;
116:             let mut result = vec![];
117:             for row in rows {
118:                 result.push(row?);
119:             }
120:             Ok(result.into_iter().next())
121:         })
122:     }
123: 
124:     pub fn find_nodes_by_kind(&self, kind: NodeKind) -> Result<Vec<Node>> {
125:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
126:             let kind_str = serde_json::to_string(&kind)?;
127:             let mut stmt = db.prepare(
128:                 "SELECT id, kind, name, properties, created_at FROM nodes WHERE kind = ?1 ORDER BY created_at DESC",
129:             )?;
130:             let rows = stmt.query_map(rusqlite::params![kind_str], row_to_node)?;
131:             let mut result = vec![];
132:             for row in rows {
133:                 result.push(row?);
134:             }
135:             Ok(result)
136:         })
137:     }
138: 
139:     pub fn find_nodes_by_kind_paginated(
140:         &self,
141:         kind: NodeKind,
142:         limit: Option<u32>,
143:         offset: Option<u32>,
144:     ) -> Result<Vec<Node>> {
145:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
146:             let kind_str = serde_json::to_string(&kind)?;
147:             let mut sql = String::from(
148:                 "SELECT id, kind, name, properties, created_at FROM nodes WHERE kind = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC",
149:             );
150:             let mut params: Vec<Box<dyn rusqlite::types::ToSql>> = vec![];
151:             params.push(Box::new(kind_str));
152:             if let Some(l) = limit {
153:                 sql.push_str(" LIMIT ?");
154:                 params.push(Box::new(l as i64));
155:             }
156:             if let Some(o) = offset {
157:                 sql.push_str(" OFFSET ?");
158:                 params.push(Box::new(o as i64));
159:             }
160:             let mut stmt = db.prepare(&sql)?;
161:             let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::types::ToSql> =
162:                 params.iter().map(|p| p.as_ref()).collect();
163:             let rows = stmt.query_map(param_refs.as_slice(), row_to_node)?;
164:             let mut result = vec![];
165:             for row in rows {
166:                 result.push(row?);
167:             }
168:             Ok(result)
169:         })
170:     }
171: 
172:     pub fn get_outgoing_edges(&self, node_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Edge>> {
173:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
174:             let stmt = db.prepare(
175:                 "SELECT id, source, target, kind, properties, created_at FROM edges WHERE source = ?1 ORDER BY created_at",
176:             )?;
177:             map_edges_query(stmt, rusqlite::params![node_id])
178:         })
179:     }
180: 
181:     pub fn get_incoming_edges(&self, node_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Edge>> {
182:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
183:             let stmt = db.prepare(
184:                 "SELECT id, source, target, kind, properties, created_at FROM edges WHERE target = ?1 ORDER BY created_at",
185:             )?;
186:             map_edges_query(stmt, rusqlite::params![node_id])
187:         })
188:     }
189: 
190:     pub fn get_dependency_chain(&self, start_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Node>> {
191:         let mut visited: HashSet<String> = HashSet::new();
192:         let mut queue = std::collections::VecDeque::new();
193:         let mut result = vec![];
194:         queue.push_back(start_id.to_string());
195:         while let Some(current) = queue.pop_front() {
196:             if !visited.insert(current.clone()) {
197:                 continue;
198:             }
199:             if let Some(node) = self.get_node(&current)? {
200:                 result.push(node);
201:             }
202:             let edges = self.get_outgoing_edges(&current)?;
203:             for edge in edges {
204:                 if matches!(edge.kind, EdgeKind::DependsOn) {
205:                     queue.push_back(edge.target);
206:                 }
207:             }
208:         }
209:         Ok(result)
210:     }
211: 
212:     pub fn find_path(&self, from: &str, to: &str) -> Result<Vec<Vec<NodeAndEdge>>> {
213:         let all_edges = self.all_edges()?;
214:         let mut adj: HashMap<String, Vec<(String, EdgeKind)>> = HashMap::new();
215:         for edge in &all_edges {
216:             adj.entry(edge.source.clone())
217:                 .or_default()
218:                 .push((edge.target.clone(), edge.kind.clone()));
219:         }
220: 
221:         type StackEntry = (String, Vec<(String, EdgeKind)>, HashSet<String>);
222:         let mut paths: Vec<Vec<(String, EdgeKind)>> = vec![];
223:         let mut stack: Vec<StackEntry> = vec![(from.to_string(), vec![], HashSet::new())];
224: 
225:         while let Some((node, path_so_far, visited)) = stack.pop() {
226:             let mut visited = visited;
227:             if node == to {
228:                 paths.push(path_so_far.clone());
229:                 continue;
230:             }
231:             if !visited.insert(node.clone()) {
232:                 continue;
233:             }
234:             if path_so_far.len() > 10 {
235:                 continue;
236:             }
237:             if let Some(neighbors) = adj.get(&node) {
238:                 for (next, kind) in neighbors {
239:                     let mut new_path = path_so_far.clone();
240:                     new_path.push((next.clone(), kind.clone()));
241:                     stack.push((next.clone(), new_path, visited.clone()));
242:                 }
243:             }
244:         }
245: 
246:         let mut result = vec![];
247:         for raw_path in &paths {
248:             let mut segment = vec![];
249:             // Add the start node (not included in DFS path traversal)
250:             if let Some(node) = self.get_node(from)? {
251:                 segment.push(NodeAndEdge {
252:                     node,
253:                     edge_kind: raw_path.first().map(|(_, ek)| ek.clone()).unwrap_or(EdgeKind::DependsOn),
254:                 });
255:             }
256:             for (node_id, edge_kind) in raw_path {
257:                 if let Some(node) = self.get_node(node_id)? {
258:                     segment.push(NodeAndEdge {
259:                         node,
260:                         edge_kind: edge_kind.clone(),
261:                     });
262:                 }
263:             }
264:             result.push(segment);
265:         }
266:         Ok(result)
267:     }
268: 
269:     pub fn all_edges(&self) -> Result<Vec<Edge>> {
270:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
271:             let stmt = db.prepare(
272:                 "SELECT id, source, target, kind, properties, created_at FROM edges ORDER BY created_at",
273:             )?;
274:             map_edges_query(stmt, [])
275:         })
276:     }
277: 
278:     pub fn all_nodes(&self) -> Result<Vec<Node>> {
279:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
280:             let mut stmt = db.prepare(
281:                 "SELECT id, kind, name, properties, created_at FROM nodes ORDER BY created_at",
282:             )?;
283:             let rows = stmt.query_map([], row_to_node)?;
284:             let mut result = vec![];
285:             for row in rows {
286:                 result.push(row?);
287:             }
288:             Ok(result)
289:         })
290:     }
291: 
292:     pub fn all_nodes_paginated(&self, limit: Option<u32>, offset: Option<u32>) -> Result<Vec<Node>> {
293:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
294:             let mut sql = String::from(
295:                 "SELECT id, kind, name, properties, created_at FROM nodes ORDER BY created_at",
296:             );
297:             let mut params: Vec<Box<dyn rusqlite::types::ToSql>> = vec![];
298:             if let Some(l) = limit {
299:                 sql.push_str(" LIMIT ?");
300:                 params.push(Box::new(l as i64));
301:             }
302:             if let Some(o) = offset {
303:                 sql.push_str(" OFFSET ?");
304:                 params.push(Box::new(o as i64));
305:             }
306:             let mut stmt = db.prepare(&sql)?;
307:             let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::types::ToSql> =
308:                 params.iter().map(|p| p.as_ref()).collect();
309:             let rows = stmt.query_map(param_refs.as_slice(), row_to_node)?;
310:             let mut result = vec![];
311:             for row in rows {
312:                 result.push(row?);
313:             }
314:             Ok(result)
315:         })
316:     }
317: 
318:     /// Find all nodes whose properties match all the given key-value pairs.
319:     /// Since SQLite stores properties as JSON text, this iterates all nodes
320:     /// and performs in-memory filtering — suitable for moderate-sized graphs.
321:     pub fn find_nodes_by_properties(
322:         &self,
323:         properties: &HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,
324:     ) -> Result<Vec<NodeAndEdge>> {
325:         with_db(&self.db, |conn| {
326:             let mut stmt = conn.prepare(
327:                 "SELECT id, kind, name, properties, created_at FROM nodes ORDER BY created_at",
328:             )?;
329:             let rows = stmt.query_map([], row_to_node)?;
330:             let mut result = vec![];
331:             for row in rows {
332:                 let node = row?;
333:                 if properties
334:                     .iter()
335:                     .all(|(k, v)| node.properties.get(k) == Some(v))
336:                 {
337:                     result.push(NodeAndEdge {
338:                         node,
339:                         edge_kind: EdgeKind::DependsOn,
340:                     });
341:                 }
342:             }
343:             Ok(result)
344:         })
345:     }
346: 
347:     /// Paginated variant of `find_nodes_by_properties`.
348:     /// Filters all nodes by properties, then applies limit/offset.
349:     pub fn find_nodes_by_properties_paginated(
350:         &self,
351:         properties: &HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,
352:         limit: usize,
353:         offset: usize,
354:     ) -> Result<Vec<NodeAndEdge>> {
355:         let all = self.find_nodes_by_properties(properties)?;
356:         let result: Vec<NodeAndEdge> = all.into_iter().skip(offset).take(limit).collect();
357:         Ok(result)
358:     }
359: 
360:     pub fn delete_node(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()> {
361:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
362:             db.execute(
363:                 "DELETE FROM edges WHERE source = ?1 OR target = ?1",
364:                 rusqlite::params![id],
365:             )?;
366:             db.execute("DELETE FROM nodes WHERE id = ?1", rusqlite::params![id])?;
367:             Ok(())
368:         })
369:     }
370: 
371:     pub fn delete_edge(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()> {
372:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
373:             db.execute("DELETE FROM edges WHERE id = ?1", rusqlite::params![id])?;
374:             Ok(())
375:         })
376:     }
377: 
378:     /// Get aggregated summary statistics about the graph.
379:     pub fn summarize(&self) -> Result<GraphSummary> {
380:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
381:             let total_nodes: u64 = db.query_row("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM nodes", [], |row| {
382:                 row.get(0)
383:             })?;
384:             let total_edges: u64 = db.query_row("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM edges", [], |row| {
385:                 row.get(0)
386:             })?;
387: 
388:             let mut nodes_by_kind: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();
389:             let mut stmt = db.prepare("SELECT kind, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM nodes GROUP BY kind")?;
390:             let rows = stmt.query_map([], |row| {
391:                 let kind: String = row.get(0)?;
392:                 let cnt: u64 = row.get(1)?;
393:                 Ok((kind.trim_matches('"').to_string(), cnt))
394:             })?;
395:             for row in rows {
396:                 let (kind, cnt) = row?;
397:                 nodes_by_kind.insert(kind, cnt);
398:             }
399: 
400:             let mut edges_by_kind: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();
401:             let mut stmt = db.prepare("SELECT kind, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM edges GROUP BY kind")?;
402:             let rows = stmt.query_map([], |row| {
403:                 let kind: String = row.get(0)?;
404:                 let cnt: u64 = row.get(1)?;
405:                 Ok((kind.trim_matches('"').to_string(), cnt))
406:             })?;
407:             for row in rows {
408:                 let (kind, cnt) = row?;
409:                 edges_by_kind.insert(kind, cnt);
410:             }
411: 
412:             Ok(GraphSummary {
413:                 total_nodes,
414:                 total_edges,
415:                 nodes_by_kind,
416:                 edges_by_kind,
417:             })
418:         })
419:     }
420: }
421: 
422: /// Summary statistics about the knowledge graph.
423: #[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize)]
424: pub struct GraphSummary {
425:     pub total_nodes: u64,
426:     pub total_edges: u64,
427:     pub nodes_by_kind: HashMap<String, u64>,
428:     pub edges_by_kind: HashMap<String, u64>,
429: }
430: 
431: fn row_to_node(row: &rusqlite::Row) -> rusqlite::Result<Node> {
432:     let props_str: String = row.get(3)?;
433:     Ok(Node {
434:         id: row.get(0)?,
435:         kind: serde_json::from_str(&row.get::<_, String>(1)?).unwrap_or(NodeKind::Module),
436:         name: row.get(2)?,
437:         properties: serde_json::from_str(&props_str).unwrap_or_default(),
438:         created_at: chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&row.get::<_, String>(4)?)
439:             .map(|dt| dt.to_utc())
440:             .unwrap_or_default(),
441:     })
442: }
443: 
444: fn map_edges_query(
445:     mut stmt: rusqlite::Statement,
446:     params: impl rusqlite::Params,
447: ) -> Result<Vec<Edge>> {
448:     let rows = stmt.query_map(params, |row| {
449:         let props_str: String = row.get(4)?;
450:         Ok(Edge {
451:             id: row.get(0)?,
452:             source: row.get(1)?,
453:             target: row.get(2)?,
454:             kind: serde_json::from_str(&row.get::<_, String>(3)?).unwrap_or(EdgeKind::DependsOn),
455:             properties: serde_json::from_str(&props_str).unwrap_or_default(),
456:             created_at: chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&row.get::<_, String>(5)?)
457:                 .map(|dt| dt.to_utc())
458:                 .unwrap_or_default(),
459:         })
460:     })?;
461:     let mut result = vec![];
462:     for row in rows {
463:         result.push(row?);
464:     }
465:     Ok(result)
466: }
467: 
468: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
469: pub struct NodeAndEdge {
470:     pub node: Node,
471:     pub edge_kind: EdgeKind,
472: }
473: 
474: #[cfg(test)]
475: mod tests {
476:     use super::*;
477:     use std::collections::HashMap;
478: 
479:     fn test_store() -> GraphStore {
480:         let dir =
481:             std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("automaton_graph_test_{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4()));
482:         std::fs::create_dir_all(&dir).unwrap();
483:         GraphStore::open(&dir).unwrap()
484:     }
485: 
486:     #[test]
487:     fn test_add_and_get_node() {
488:         let store = test_store();
489:         let mut props = HashMap::new();
490:         props.insert("key".to_string(), serde_json::json!("value"));
491:         let id = store
492:             .add_node(NodeKind::Module, "test.module", props)
493:             .unwrap();
494:         assert!(!id.is_empty());
495: 
496:         let node = store.get_node(&id).unwrap().expect("Node should exist");
497:         assert_eq!(node.name, "test.module");
498:         assert_eq!(node.kind, NodeKind::Module);
499:     }
500: 
501:     #[test]
502:     fn test_add_and_get_edge() {
503:         let store = test_store();
504:         let a = store
505:             .add_node(NodeKind::Module, "mod.a", HashMap::new())
506:             .unwrap();
507:         let b = store
508:             .add_node(NodeKind::Module, "mod.b", HashMap::new())
509:             .unwrap();
510:         let eid = store
511:             .add_edge(&a, &b, EdgeKind::DependsOn, HashMap::new())
512:             .unwrap();
513:         assert!(!eid.is_empty());
514: 
515:         let outgoing = store.get_outgoing_edges(&a).unwrap();
516:         assert_eq!(outgoing.len(), 1);
517:         assert_eq!(outgoing[0].target, b);
518: 
519:         let incoming = store.get_incoming_edges(&b).unwrap();
520:         assert_eq!(incoming.len(), 1);
521:         assert_eq!(incoming[0].source, a);
522:     }
523: 
524:     #[test]
525:     fn test_find_nodes_by_kind() {
526:         let store = test_store();
527:         store
528:             .add_node(NodeKind::Module, "mod.a", HashMap::new())
529:             .unwrap();
530:         store
531:             .add_node(NodeKind::Trigger, "trig.x", HashMap::new())
532:             .unwrap();
533:         store
534:             .add_node(NodeKind::Workflow, "flow.y", HashMap::new())
535:             .unwrap();
536: 
537:         let modules = store.find_nodes_by_kind(NodeKind::Module).unwrap();
538:         assert_eq!(modules.len(), 1);
539:         assert_eq!(modules[0].name, "mod.a");
540:     }
541: 
542:     #[test]
543:     fn test_delete_node() {
544:         let store = test_store();
545:         let id = store
546:             .add_node(NodeKind::Module, "to_delete", HashMap::new())
547:             .unwrap();
548:         store.delete_node(&id).unwrap();
549:         assert!(store.get_node(&id).unwrap().is_none());
550:     }
551: 
552:     #[test]
553:     fn test_delete_edge() {
554:         let store = test_store();
555:         let a = store
556:             .add_node(NodeKind::Module, "a", HashMap::new())
557:             .unwrap();
558:         let b = store
559:             .add_node(NodeKind::Module, "b", HashMap::new())
560:             .unwrap();
561:         let eid = store
562:             .add_edge(&a, &b, EdgeKind::Calls, HashMap::new())
563:             .unwrap();
564:         store.delete_edge(&eid).unwrap();
565:         assert_eq!(store.get_outgoing_edges(&a).unwrap().len(), 0);
566:     }
567: 
568:     #[test]
569:     fn test_dependency_chain() {
570:         let store = test_store();
571:         let a = store
572:             .add_node(NodeKind::Module, "root", HashMap::new())
573:             .unwrap();
574:         let b = store
575:             .add_node(NodeKind::Module, "dep1", HashMap::new())
576:             .unwrap();
577:         let c = store
578:             .add_node(NodeKind::Module, "dep2", HashMap::new())
579:             .unwrap();
580:         store
581:             .add_edge(&a, &b, EdgeKind::DependsOn, HashMap::new())
582:             .unwrap();
583:         store
584:             .add_edge(&b, &c, EdgeKind::DependsOn, HashMap::new())
585:             .unwrap();
586: 
587:         let chain = store.get_dependency_chain(&a).unwrap();
588:         assert_eq!(chain.len(), 3);
589:         // root, dep1, dep2 in traversal order
590:         let names: Vec<&str> = chain.iter().map(|n| n.name.as_str()).collect();
591:         assert!(names.contains(&"root"));
592:         assert!(names.contains(&"dep1"));
593:         assert!(names.contains(&"dep2"));
594:     }
595: 
596:     #[test]
597:     fn test_pathfinding() {
598:         let store = test_store();
599:         let a = store
600:             .add_node(NodeKind::Module, "start", HashMap::new())
601:             .unwrap();
602:         let b = store
603:             .add_node(NodeKind::Module, "mid", HashMap::new())
604:             .unwrap();
605:         let c = store
606:             .add_node(NodeKind::Module, "end", HashMap::new())
607:             .unwrap();
608:         store
609:             .add_edge(&a, &b, EdgeKind::DependsOn, HashMap::new())
610:             .unwrap();
611:         store
612:             .add_edge(&b, &c, EdgeKind::DependsOn, HashMap::new())
613:             .unwrap();
614: 
615:         let paths = store.find_path(&a, &c).unwrap();
616:         assert!(!paths.is_empty());
617:     }
618: }
